label: offensive
rationale: 'Political harlots', 'skunks and charlatans' - personal abuse aimed at named individual politicians (Wandayi, Atandis, Sudis, Ichungwa, Murkomen), not transferred onto any ethnic community.

label: offensive
rationale: 'radical Islamist simp' targets a named individual (Corbyn) with a personal insult; though 'Islamist' has a religious charge, the attack is on the individual's alleged sympathies rather than on Muslims as a group.

label: neither
rationale: Ethnic-bloc electoral arithmetic ('his fellow luhya is about to be president') combined with criticism of a named individual ('yeye ni bangi tu'); no collective contempt attributed to the Luhya community.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kenyan born Muslim, terrorist Obama' dehumanises and collectively smears Muslims by coupling religion with terrorism to attack a named public figure via his attributed group identity.

label: hate
rationale: 'Down right low class impoverished Kikuyu mentality' attributes a collective, immutable negative characteristic to the entire Kikuyu community - NCIC ethnic contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Describes Kikuyu political figures uniting against Ruto; ethnic-bloc coalition analysis of named politicians, no collective contempt attributed to the community.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyu's will descend on you like vultures descending on a carcass' applies a dehumanising predator metaphor to the entire Kikuyu community as a collective, going beyond criticism of named politicians.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Tribal bigot and corrupt polishtian' directed at named individuals Rigathi and William - personal insults on politicians, not transferred onto their ethnic communities.

label: neither
rationale: Ordinary Sheng banter comparing friend groups; 'mbogi' used in its everyday sense with no ethnic target or harmful intent.

label: hate
rationale: 'All Kikuyus ... will be bought by Ruto' attributes an immutable collective characteristic - corruptibility/venality - to every member of the Kikuyu community, NCIC collective ethnic contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Observational statement about inter-ethnic marriage patterns among Kalenjin MPs; no contempt, dehumanisation, or attack on the community.

label: hate
rationale: 'Wajaluo mnajifanya mmesoma' ('Luos pretend they are educated') attributes collective pretension/foolishness to the entire Luo community, NCIC ethnic contempt even if framed as a 'lecture'.

label: hate
rationale: 'Enyewe waluhya wengine ni wapumbavu' ('some Luhyas are truly stupid') attributes collective stupidity to a subset of the Luhya community - NCIC collective ethnic contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Observational anecdote about a Kipsigis individual's position on Ruto's term; no collective contempt attributed to the Kipsigis community.

label: neither
rationale: Criticism of a named politician (Ichungwa) and relief they did not serve in government; no ethnic community is targeted.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyu HEGEMONY merchants will always unite and mislead a very innocent community' attributes a collective, immutable pattern of deception and domination-seeking to the entire Kikuyu community.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyus can decide whether to continue with the Wantam slogan or advocate for secession' frames the entire Kikuyu community as a disloyal bloc to be expelled from national belonging, NCIC collective ethnic contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'wantam mongrels' - 'mongrels' is a dehumanising slur applied collectively to the Kikuyu-associated wantam bloc, going beyond criticism of a political position to degrade a community.

label: offensive
rationale: 'CHIEF GOON' directed at named individual Murkomen; 'goon' here is political abuse of a specific official, no ethnic group targeted.

label: neither
rationale: Vernacular comment that a named Luhya figure is eating well and is satisfied; intra-community banter about a politician, no collective contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Condemns Hamas and cited atrocities in the context of debating Corbyn's position; hostile framing but directed at a named armed organisation's acts rather than Muslims as a collective group.

label: hate
rationale: 'LUOS DON'T WANT KIKUYUS PUPPETS' combined with 'BY FIRE OR BULLETS' constitutes both ethnic exclusion framing and an explicit violence call against Kikuyu-aligned figures in Kisumu.

label: hate
rationale: 'Somalis with stolen Minnesota money' attributes collective criminality to Somalis, and 'Nairobi Muslim Academy ikipelekwa Mogadishu' implies expulsion of a Muslim institution, combining ethnic targeting with a veiled expulsion call.

label: hate
rationale: 'hao ndio hua hawapigii mtu wao and most confused' attributes collective disloyalty and confusion to identified Luhya sub-groups as a community, NCIC collective ethnic contempt.

label: neither
rationale: A one-word question ('Na mkisii?') asking about the Kisii community in what appears to be a voting-pattern discussion; no contempt or attack expressed.
